translations.demo_banner: Demo mode — sample data, no real car.
translations.demo_exit: Exit demo & set up my car
translations.reev_subtitle: Fuel and combined-range sensors for range-extender models (B10 / C10 REEV).
translations.reev_beta_note: Beta / data-collection view. These are the raw fuel signals read live from the car — they are not yet used in trips, charges or statistics. Once the data is validated they'll be woven into the other pages.
translations.reev_consumption_note: From the car's own cloud data (recent weeks): electric kWh/100km and fuel L/100km.
translations.reev_demo_banner: Sample data — layout preview, not read from a real car.
translations.reev_engine_km_hint: the L/100 km is over this distance, not the whole trip
translations.reev_elec_source_note: Electric from the car's metered getEC, not the battery % (a running generator recharges the pack mid-drive, so ΔSoC isn't what the motor used).
translations.reev_elec_7d_note: Metered by the car (getEC): driving + climate + other — the kWh counterpart to the litres above.
translations.reev_engine_consumption_note: How much fuel the range-extender burns when it actually runs — from your own trips, not the cloud's all-km average (which a mostly-electric REEV dilutes to near zero).
translations.reev_no_data: This view is for range-extender (REEV) models. If you have a REEV and just set it up, the sensors appear after the next poll.
translations.reev_fetch_fail: Couldn't read live signals from the car right now — try again in a moment.
translations.reev_raw_note: The exact values read from the car, with their signal IDs — handy for cross-checking against your official-app screens.
translations.beta_banner: BETA TESTER BUILD — data on Statistics, Costs, Trips and other pages may be INACCURATE (REEV support is not integrated yet). Please do NOT open issues about inconsistent data. This build only captures data to build future REEV support in Mate.
translations.reev_research_note: This build captures every raw vehicle signal so we can map REEV behaviour and ship real REEV support. Add logbook notes for anything notable (e.g. the engine started to charge while driving), then export the encrypted bundle and attach it to a beta issue together with your official-app screenshots.
translations.reev_logbook_note: Timestamped notes paired with the captured signals — the key to decoding REEV.
translations.reev_logbook_ph: e.g. refueled to 100% / engine started to charge while driving
translations.reev_export_note: GPS is stripped and the bundle is encrypted so only the maintainer can open it. Attach it to a beta issue.
translations.consent_body: This is a beta-tester build whose purpose is to build REEV support for Mate. It captures all of your vehicle's raw signals locally and lets you export an encrypted bundle to send for analysis. Your data stays on your device until you choose to export it and attach it to an issue — GPS is removed and the bundle can only be opened by the maintainer. Note that Statistics, Costs and Trips may be inaccurate in this build, so please do not open issues about inconsistent data. By continuing you agree to take part in this data collection.
translations.map_subtitle: Everywhere you've driven, and your most-visited places
translations.map_no_data: No GPS data yet — drive a bit and your map will fill in.
translations.battery_subtitle: Estimated usable capacity over time, from real charge energy vs the SoC gained
translations.battery_soh_cold_note: Faded points are cold charges (below {n} °C): shown for reference but left out of the figure — LFP reads low when cold. Charges ending near 100% count the most, where the BMS recalibrates the SoC.
translations.battery_soh_active_use_note: Orange points are charges where the car was on and consuming power during the session — the energy/SoC ratio is distorted by onboard loads, so they're shown for reference but excluded from the health figure.
translations.battery_soh_soc_jump_note: Orange points with a BMS recalibration label had a sudden SoC snap during charging — the BMS corrected its SoC estimate without matching energy, inflating the delta and deflating the capacity estimate. Shown for reference, excluded from the figure.
translations.battery_soh_low_start_note: Violet points are charges that started below {n}% — near-empty, the BMS over-reports the SoC rise, so this reads as a capacity that's too low. Shown for reference, excluded from the figure.
translations.battery_method: Each point is the measured energy delivered during a charge (∫ voltage × current) divided by the SoC it added — an estimate of full-pack capacity. Only charges with a meaningful SoC rise and stored telemetry are used; single points are noisy, so the headline figure is averaged over the most recent charges. This is an estimate, not a lab measurement.
translations.battery_vampire_subtitle: Charge lost from power-off to the next power-on — includes heating/cooling while the car is off.
translations.battery_vampire_below_hint: {n} parked window(s) recorded drain below your {thr}%/day display threshold — lower it in Settings → Advanced to chart them.
translations.battery_vampire_active_use_note: {n} window(s) include heavy off-state heating/cooling (shown in amber, counted in the drain).
translations.battery_vampire_help: Each bar is a stretch the car sat parked and unplugged for at least an hour; the value is the SoC it lost, normalised to %/day. Stretches with any driving (speed or an odometer change) or charging are excluded, and tiny drops are treated as sensor noise. Pale bars are short or still-running parks, where a single 0.1% sensor step can inflate the daily rate — the ± in the tooltip shows by how much. The headline is total SoC lost ÷ total parked time across all parks, including those that lost nothing. Computed automatically from logged telemetry — no input needed.
translations.report_subtitle: Driving, charging and cost — one month at a glance
translations.report_no_data_hint: Your monthly report appears after the first trip or charge.
translations.nav_subtitle: Send a destination to the car's navigation
translations.geocoder_desc: By default Mate looks up addresses with a free OpenStreetMap-based provider. Pick a provider + API key below for better street and house-number coverage.
translations.geocoder_key_hint: Geoapify is free with no credit card and includes house numbers — get a key at myprojects.geoapify.com. Leave the provider on “Keyless” to use OpenStreetMap.
translations.charger_locator_desc: Names the public charging station of each charge (📍 in the charge list and on the last charge) using OpenStreetMap. The charge GPS position is sent to the public Overpass service for the lookup — home charges are never sent. Already-recorded charges are filled in too.
translations.charger_locator_enable: Label charges with the station name
translations.charger_locator_started: ✓ Saved — looking up your past charges in the background
translations.charger_locator_ocm_label: Open Charge Map API key (optional, recommended)
translations.charger_locator_ocm_hint: Free key at openchargemap.org — adds the EV-dedicated Open Charge Map registry on top of OpenStreetMap: more stations, with AC/DC and kW for each. The two sources complement each other.
translations.charger_locator_tomtom_hint: Free key at developer.tomtom.com (2,500 requests/day) — adds TomTom's commercial coverage, useful abroad and for brand-new stations. Used only for the “Find chargers” view, not for the saved labels.
translations.nav_chargers_list_title: Stations found (tap to locate on the map)
translations.nav_chargers_found: stations found — tap a pin to set it as destination
translations.nav_chargers_none: No charging station within the selected radius
translations.nav_chargers_widened: None within the chosen radius — nearest ones instead:
translations.nav_chargers_error: Station service unreachable — try again shortly
translations.nav_chargers_no_pos: Car position unknown — no GPS fix yet
translations.nav_not_realtime: live availability not shown (OSM map data)
translations.charge_detect_desc: Minimum charging current to count as charging. Below this, a plugged-in car is treated as idle (no session logged). Raise it to ignore very low / phantom currents, lower it to catch trickle charging.
translations.adv_desc: Fine-tuning for edge cases. The defaults suit most users — change these only if you know why.
translations.adv_reconstruct_help: Minimum battery-% jump (while parked) that Mate logs as a charge it missed live — for charges that happened while the car was asleep. Lower catches smaller top-ups but risks phantom charges from sensor noise (floor 1%).
translations.adv_vampire_help: Parked SoC drops smaller than this are treated as sensor noise and ignored in the vampire-drain chart.
translations.adv_vampire_hours_help: Parked stretches shorter than this are hidden from the chart — a brief stop extrapolates a single SoC step into a large %/day rate.
translations.adv_dc_help: Charges above this power are classified DC (fast charging). Raise it if you have a 22 kW AC wallbox so its sessions aren't labelled DC.
translations.adv_soh_temp_help: Charges whose minimum battery temperature is below this are shown but excluded from the State-of-Health estimate — a cold LFP pack reads low and would understate health. Set to 0 to include every charge.
translations.wallbox_enable_desc: Show a Wallbox page in the sidebar with live data from Home Assistant
translations.wallbox_auto_home_desc: Charges where the wallbox measured energy are confirmed as Home automatically — cost computed from your prices and time bands, exactly like a manual confirm
translations.wallbox_not_connected_desc: Once connected, this page will show live charging power, session statistics and a car-vs-wallbox comparison chart.
translations.ha_token_hint: Created in HA → Profile → Security → Long-Lived Access Tokens
translations.ha_token_set: saved — leave empty to keep it
translations.ha_supervisor_note: Connected automatically through the Home Assistant add-on (Supervisor).
translations.wb_keywords_hint: Comma-separated keywords to detect wallbox entities (e.g. wallbox, charger, evse, keba, pulsar). Custom keywords replace the automatic device-class detection. Leave empty to use defaults.
translations.wb_max_power_static_hint: Used when no sensor is mapped above — your wallbox's max power/current. Display only, it never affects cost.
translations.wb_picker_help: Mate auto-detects charger entities by name and device-class. If yours aren't listed, tick “Show all entities” below — or add your wallbox's name to the detection keywords.
translations.wb_role_power_hint: Live charging power the wallbox delivers — a ‘sensor’ in W or kW. Drives the AC-vs-DC chart.
translations.wb_role_energy_hint: Energy added during the session — a ‘sensor’ in kWh. Used for your home-charge cost.
translations.wb_role_status_hint: The wallbox state — a ‘sensor’ showing text like charging / connected / idle.
translations.wb_role_max_current_hint: The control that sets the charging current — a ‘number’ entity in A (not a sensor).
translations.wb_role_max_power_hint: Max available charging limit, e.g. from dynamic load balancing — a ‘sensor’, in kW or A depending on the wallbox (V2C/Pulsar report A).
translations.wb_b10_unplugged: B10 not connected — session data may be from another vehicle
translations.wb_profile_save_hint: Save the current configuration under a name to easily switch between wallboxes.
translations.wb_profile_load_confirm: Switch to profile "{name}"? The current HA connection and entity mapping will be replaced.
translations.wb_profile_no_profiles: No saved profiles yet — save the current configuration to create one.
translations.wb_profile_name_required: Enter a name for this profile
translations.wb_profile_name_taken: A profile with that name already exists
translations.wb_profile_charging_blocked: Cannot switch profiles while a charge is active
translations.charge_free_hint: Mark this home charge as free — e.g. self-produced solar. It stays under Home, at cost 0. Mate can't measure the solar/grid split, so this is your call.
translations.cost_basis_ac: Cost billed on the wallbox AC energy (conversion losses included).
translations.cost_basis_dc: Cost billed on battery DC energy — wallbox AC history unavailable for this charge.
translations.cost_basis_manual: Cost entered manually (the real total you paid) — overrides the automatic estimate.
translations.abrp_desc: Send live telemetry to A Better Route Planner for live route planning (opt‑in).
translations.abrp_token_hint: ABRP app → Settings → Car → Live Data (Generic). Nothing is sent until enabled.
translations.mqtt_desc: Expose the car to Home Assistant as native entities (sensors + commands) via MQTT discovery.
translations.retention_hint: Older non‑charging GPS points are pruned daily; trips and charge curves are always kept.
translations.restore_db_hint: Moving to a new install? Download the backup above, reinstall the add-on and sign in, then upload that file here to bring ALL your data back — nothing is lost, and the login you just entered is kept.
translations.restore_db_confirm: This REPLACES all current data with the backup file. Continue?
translations.restore_db_ok: Database restored — the add-on is restarting, this page reloads in ~20s.
translations.export_db_hint: Download your data. The database backup also contains your encrypted credentials — keep secret.key with it to restore on another install.
translations.wb_max_current_hint: The wallbox entity that adjusts the charging current/power (a 'number' entity)
translations.wb_automation_note: Your HA automations may override this value.
translations.wallbox_compare_hint: See it per session in the Charges page, inside each charge's power chart.
translations.wallbox_compare_legend: kWh delivered vs into the battery (per session, rolled up by day/month/year)
translations.vehicle_subtitle: Live tyres, doors, windows and temperatures
translations.cmd_timeout_car: Sent — the car didn't confirm in time (weak signal or asleep). It may still have worked; try again shortly.
translations.cmd_cloud_unreach: Couldn't reach the Leapmotor cloud — check your connection and retry.
translations.car_resp_hint: How reliably your car answers commands lately — a proxy for its mobile coverage where it's parked. Measured only when you send commands.
translations.check_poller: Make sure the poller is running
translations.trip_delete_confirm: Delete this trip permanently? This can't be undone.
translations.charge_delete_confirm: Delete this charge session permanently? This can't be undone.
translations.note_ph_charge: Station location, shade, reliability, parking, weather, personal remarks…
translations.note_ph_trip: Traffic, weather, road type, personal remarks…
translations.drive_settings_hint: Manual tags — the car doesn't report drive mode or One-Pedal to the cloud.
translations.trip_defaults_desc: The car never reports drive mode or One-Pedal to the cloud, so they're tagged by hand. If you always drive the same way, set your default here and every new trip starts pre-filled — no need to tag each one.
translations.trip_defaults_hint: Applies to new trips only; existing trips are untouched. You can still change any single trip afterwards.
translations.prep_off_confirm: Turn A/C, seats, steering and mirror heating all off?
translations.charge_reconstructed_hint: Recorded from the battery-level jump — the car was asleep during this charge, so its exact timing and power weren't reported.
translations.trip_reconstructed_hint: Recorded from the odometer jump — the car was offline to the cloud during this drive, so there's no GPS route and its exact timing may be approximate.
translations.diag_hint: If something isn't working, copy or download these and attach them to a GitHub issue — it helps us pinpoint the problem fast.
translations.diag_privacy: The downloadable diagnostics mask your personal info (VIN, credentials) and strip your GPS location from the raw signals — so the whole bundle is safe to share publicly.
translations.missed_scan_desc: Scans your history for charges that happened while the car was asleep (before automatic detection existed) and were never logged. Shows you what it finds before adding anything.
translations.missed_none: No missed charges found in your history. 👍
translations.prep_intro: Pre-condition your car with one touch — now or on a schedule.
translations.prep_sched_desc: The car starts at the set time and stops after ~20 minutes.
translations.prep_sched_hint: Times use the vehicle's local timezone; the start is anchored to the next future occurrence.
translations.ready_desc: The moment the car goes Ready, run this same preparation automatically — no schedule, no tapping “Prepare now”.
translations.ready_temp_gate: Only when the interior temperature is
translations.ready_temp_hint: No external-temperature sensor exists in the cloud API — this reads the cabin's own sensor. Leave unchecked to run on every power-on.
translations.ready_hint: Fires once per power-on session, not repeatedly — and never re-fires for a later drive within the same session (e.g. a stop without switching off). Runs on the real car the next time it's actually turned on.
translations.charge_limit_desc: Max SOC the car will charge to
translations.sched_charge_desc: Charge only inside a time window (e.g. off-peak)
translations.sched_climate_none: No climate schedule set on the car
translations.sched_climate_hint: Mate manages a single climate schedule — saving here replaces the car's current one
translations.sched_charge_hint: Mate manages the charge schedule — saving here replaces the car's current one
translations.sched_climate_desc: Pre-condition the cabin before you leave
translations.manual_charge_hint: Backfill charging sessions from before you started using Mate, so the lifetime totals and monthly report are complete. No SoC or power curve — just the totals.
translations.import_hint:
Download the template, add one charge per row, then upload it here.
Columns: date (YYYY-MM-DD), energy_kwh, cost (optional), type (AC or DC), start_soc, end_soc (%, optional), end (date/time). Lines starting with # are ignored.
translations.import_none: No valid charges found in the file.
translations.plug_in_car: Plug in your car and charges will appear here
translations.energy_in_battery_help: The energy that actually entered the battery (DC). What you draw from the grid is ~10–15% higher (AC→DC conversion losses); without a wallbox reading, Mate can only show this figure.
translations.merge_help: Join two trips that a short, non-charging stop split apart. Adjust the max stop; pairs that charged in between are never shown.
translations.refresh_help: Pull the car's current state now instead of waiting for the next ~30s update. Mate reads passively — it won't wake a sleeping car.
translations.merge_none: No mergeable trips at this gap.
translations.merge_failed: Couldn't merge (a charge happened in the gap, or it's too long).
translations.wallbox_billed_help: Home charges are billed on the energy the wallbox drew (AC, conversion losses included), never less than what reached the battery.
translations.no_power_data: No power data for this session
translations.clim_on_confirm: Note: once on, Mate can't fully turn the climate off — you'll need the Leapmotor app, or do it in the car. Turn it on?
translations.clim_off_note: Heads-up: turning a function off returns the climate to its base mode — it does not fully power off the A/C. To switch the climate off completely, use the Leapmotor app or do it manually in the car.
translations.vehicle_driving_locked: Car in motion — these controls are disabled
translations.unlock_charger_hint: Releases the cable locked in the charge port
translations.ec_note: Official Leapmotor driving consumption (drive + climate while driving). Parked/standby energy is not included; granularity is whole solar days.
translations.ec_trips_partial: Distance, duration and average are hidden: Mate has only been recording trips since {d}, while this window starts earlier — the cloud total covers the car's whole period, local trips don't.
translations.ec_trip_note: Driving energy from the cloud (official) — replaces the SoC estimate for new trips.
translations.ec_provisional: Provisional estimate from SoC — waiting for the official cloud figure.
translations.ec_convert_nodata: The cloud has no official breakdown for this trip — the SoC estimate above is used.
translations.ec_convert_merged: The cloud merged this trip with the previous one (stop too short): merge the two trips to recover the data.
translations.ec_convert_implausible: The cloud's official figure for this trip looks unreliable (it would imply an impossible consumption — common on very short trips), so the SoC estimate above is kept.
translations.ec_convert_shared: The car was never switched off between this trip and the adjacent one, so the cloud counts them as a single session. Merge the trips to get the real official consumption.
translations.ec_revert_confirm: Revert this trip to the SoC estimate? You can convert it again later.
translations.cum_note: Official Leapmotor cumulative figure. 'Standby' is estimated = total − (driving + A/C + other) from getEC.
translations.stats_after_trip: Statistics will appear after your first trip
translations.account_logout_hint: Sign out to link a different Leapmotor account — your trips and charges stay.
translations.account_logout_confirm: Log out of Leapmotor? You'll set up again with another account. Your saved trips, charges and positions are kept (for the same car).
translations.account_reset_hint: Permanently erase everything and start over — wipes the account, all trips, charges, positions and settings.
translations.account_reset_warn: This cannot be undone. All your history and settings will be lost and the setup wizard reopens as a fresh install. The app certificate is kept.
translations.polling_desc: How often Mate reads the cloud. Faster = trips & charges caught sooner (polling does NOT drain the car). Slower = fewer API calls.
translations.capacity_desc: Usable battery capacity, used for energy calculations. Pre-filled per model; edit it if yours differs or to match a measured value.
translations.charge_prices_desc: Cost per kWh for each charge type — used to calculate session costs automatically.
translations.ac_desc: Public AC stations up to 22 kW
translations.fast_desc: DC fast chargers up to 100 kW
translations.costs_desc: Set how much each kWh costs, so Mate can price your charging sessions.
translations.cost_mode_per_type_hint: Pick a mode for each charge type — e.g. home on your dynamic tariff sensor, public AC/DC/HPC on their operators' fixed prices.
translations.cost_mode_flat_desc: One price per charge type, all day long.
translations.cost_mode_tou_desc: Different prices depending on the time of day.
translations.cost_mode_dynamic_desc: Price from a Home Assistant sensor that changes over time (e.g. Nordpool, Tibber).
translations.cost_dynamic_base_note: Also used whenever the sensor is unreachable or has no data for a session.
translations.cost_dynamic_fallback_note: Fallback price — used only if the sensor is unreachable or has no data, or while a charge is still in progress.
translations.dyn_price_desc: Pick the Home Assistant sensor with your electricity price (e.g. Nordpool, Tibber, or your utility's own integration). Mate reads its history for each session and prices every interval by the value in effect at that moment.
translations.dyn_picker_help: Only entities that look like a price (by name or a per-kWh currency unit) are listed. If yours is missing, check its unit_of_measurement in Home Assistant.
translations.dyn_no_entities: No price-like entity found in Home Assistant.
translations.cost_base_note: Also used for hours not covered by any band.
translations.tou_method_split_desc: Splits each session's energy across bands using the real power curve.
translations.tou_method_start_desc: Prices the whole session by the band it started in.
translations.tou_bands_desc: Add one or more bands across the day; set a price per charge type for each. Bands may cross midnight (e.g. 23:30 → 06:30).
translations.no_bands: No bands yet — add one to get started.
translations.costs_future_note: Cost changes apply to future charges only — costs already calculated are not modified.
translations.band_blank_hint: The greyed-out price is the base price and applies automatically. Type a value only to change it for that band, or 0 if it's free.
translations.setup_subtitle: Configure your vehicle connection to get started
translations.units_hint: Display only — your stored data stays metric.
